patient waiting loop with small motion

pet_notes: Eric Cartman from South Park as a compact Codex digital pet. Preserve his recognizable round face, blue hat with yellow puff, red jacket, yellow mittens, brown pants, black shoes, and smug expressive personality. Actions should feel like South Park paper cutout animation: stiff-body sliding steps, minimal hinged limb movement, simple mouth/eye changes, deadpan exaggerated expressions. State behavior should match Cartman's persona: idle smug and impatient, running as a waddling slide-step, waving as a reluctant smug hand wave, jumping as a short annoyed hop, failed as a furious tantrum or offended sulk, waiting as impatient foot-tap and side-eye, review as suspicious squinting judgement.
style_notes: South Park-inspired flat paper cutout cartoon style adapted to a Codex pet atlas. Use clean official-cartoon-like hard-edged shapes, simple flat fills, thick black outline, no pixelated block rendering, no painterly shading, no gradients, no 3D, no glossy lighting. Keep the sprite readable at 192x208 with compact proportions, but do not turn it into pixel art. No text, no logos, no scenery, no shadows, no speed lines, no detached effects, flat chroma-key background.
house_style: Codex digital pet sprite style: pixel-art-adjacent low-resolution mascot sprite, compact chibi proportions, chunky whole-body silhouette, thick dark 1-2 px outline, visible stepped/pixel edges, limited palette, flat cel shading with at most one small highlight and one shadow step, simple readable face, tiny limbs, and no detail that disappears at 192x208. Avoid polished illustration, painterly rendering, anime key art, 3D render, vector app-icon polish, glossy lighting, soft gradients, realistic fur or material texture, anti-aliased high-detail edges, and complex tiny accessories.
